title: Fascia-directed interventions and emotional / psychological outcomes in humans
question: In humans, do interventions that explicitly target the fascial / connective-tissue system (e.g. myofascial release, fascial manipulation, connective-tissue manipulation, structural integration) change emotional states, affect regulation, or psychological outcomes (anxiety, stress, depression, trauma), as measured by validated psychological instruments or physiological affect markers?

pico.population: Human participants of any age, healthy or clinical (e.g. chronic pain, anxiety, PTSD, fibromyalgia)
pico.exposure: An intervention explicitly framed as targeting fascia / myofascial / connective tissue (myofascial release or induction, fascial manipulation, connective-tissue manipulation/massage, structural integration/Rolfing, fascia-directed manual therapy)
pico.comparator: Sham, no-treatment, waitlist, active control, or within-subject pre/post (single-arm allowed but flagged)
pico.outcome: Emotional / psychological outcome measured with a validated psychological instrument (e.g. STAI, PSS, PHQ, BDI, PCL, POMS) or a physiological affect marker (cortisol, heart-rate variability, skin conductance)

inclusion_criteria: Human participants (any age; healthy or clinical population)
Applies an intervention explicitly framed as targeting fascia / myofascial / connective tissue (not generic massage, exercise, or relaxation lacking a fascial rationale)
Reports at least one emotional / psychological outcome measured with a validated psychological instrument or a physiological affect marker (cortisol, HRV, skin conductance)
Primary study reporting original data: RCT, non-randomised trial, controlled or single-arm pre/post interventional, or experimental design

fields.label: Study design
fields.help: RCT, non-randomised trial, controlled pre/post, single-arm pre/post, crossover

fields.label: Population
fields.help: healthy volunteers, or clinical group (e.g. chronic low-back pain, fibromyalgia, anxiety); include n

fields.label: Fascia intervention
fields.help: the fascia-directed technique as named by the study (e.g. myofascial release, structural integration)

fields.label: Comparator
fields.help: sham, waitlist, active control, or none (single-arm)

fields.label: Outcome measure(s)
fields.help: the validated instrument(s) or physiological marker(s) reported

fields.label: Result
fields.help: quote the study's own numbers / effect sizes / p-values for the affect outcome

fields.label: Notes
fields.help: dose/sessions, blinding, dropouts, caveats, physiological vs self-report divergence

searches.query: ("myofascial release" OR "myofascial induction" OR "fascial manipulation" OR "connective tissue manipulation" OR "connective tissue massage" OR "structural integration" OR "fascial release" OR "fascia-directed") AND (anxiety OR stress OR depression OR mood OR affect OR emotional OR emotion OR psychological OR distress OR wellbeing OR "well-being")

("myofascial release" OR "myofascial induction" OR "fascial manipulation" OR "connective tissue manipulation" OR "connective tissue massage" OR "structural integration" OR "fascial" OR "myofascial therapy") AND (cortisol OR "heart rate variability" OR "skin conductance" OR "autonomic" OR "parasympathetic" OR "salivary" OR "vagal")

(Rolfing OR "structural integration" OR "myofascial induction" OR "Bowen technique" OR "Bowen therapy" OR "fascial manipulation" OR "fascia" OR "myofascial") AND (anxiety OR stress OR depression OR mood OR "quality of life" OR psychological OR "mental health" OR trauma OR PTSD OR wellbeing)

(Rolfing OR "structural integration" OR "Bowen technique" OR "Bowen therapy" OR "fascial manipulation" OR "myofascial induction" OR "myofascial release" OR "fascial unwinding") AND (anxiety OR stress OR depression OR mood OR affect OR emotion OR psychological OR "mental health" OR trauma OR PTSD OR wellbeing OR "quality of life")
